quadratic_11.label: Quadratic Functions & Complex Numbers
misconceptions: Complex solutions mean the quadratic has no solutions
i² is equal to -1, not that i is the square root of -1
Vertex form always requires completing the square
quadratic_11.advanced: Solves quadratic equations over the complex numbers. Writes equations given roots (real and/or complex). Analyzes quadratic functions fully using vertex form and discriminant. Interprets complex solutions in context and applies quadratics to model complex scenarios.
quadratic_11.proficient: Completes the square to convert to vertex form. Uses the quadratic formula for complex solutions. Performs operations with complex numbers. Interprets the discriminant and analyzes parabola properties.
quadratic_11.developing: Solves quadratics using the quadratic formula. Performs simple operations with complex numbers. Identifies vertex and key features of parabolas with support.
quadratic_11.beginning: Recognizes quadratic equations and complex numbers. Can perform very basic operations. Cannot yet complete the square or fully analyze quadratic functions independently.

polynomial_11.label: Polynomial Functions
misconceptions: End behavior depends only on degree, not leading coefficient sign
All rational roots must be integers
A polynomial of degree n has exactly n distinct roots
polynomial_11.advanced: Analyzes polynomial functions using end behavior, zeros with multiplicity, and graphs. Applies the Rational Root Theorem and Factor Theorem to find all roots. Uses synthetic division efficiently. Proves properties of polynomials algebraically.
polynomial_11.proficient: Determines end behavior from the leading term. Finds zeros and identifies multiplicity. Uses synthetic division and applies the Rational Root Theorem. Sketches graphs incorporating all key features.
polynomial_11.developing: Identifies degree and leading term. Finds some zeros using factoring or the given information. Applies synthetic division with support. Sketches basic polynomial graphs.
polynomial_11.beginning: Recognizes polynomials and their degree. Cannot yet reliably find zeros or apply techniques for analysis independently.

exponential_11.label: Exponential & Logarithmic Functions
misconceptions: An exponential equation always has exactly one solution
Logarithms with different bases cannot be compared
exponential_11.advanced: Solves exponential and logarithmic equations using properties and algebraic techniques. Creates exponential and logarithmic models for real-world data. Interprets parameters in exponential models. Proves logarithm properties and applies them to complex expressions.
exponential_11.proficient: Solves exponential equations by expressing both sides with the same base or using logarithms. Solves logarithmic equations. Expands and condenses logarithmic expressions. Applies exponential growth and decay models.
exponential_11.developing: Evaluates exponential and logarithmic functions. Solves simple exponential equations. Applies one logarithm property at a time with support.
exponential_11.beginning: Evaluates basic exponential and logarithmic expressions. Cannot yet solve equations or apply multiple properties independently.

rational_11.label: Rational Expressions & Equations
misconceptions: Vertical asymptotes and holes are the same
Factoring the numerator and denominator changes the function
Horizontal asymptotes are always at y = 1
rational_11.advanced: Simplifies complex rational expressions and identifies holes and asymptotes. Solves rational equations and identifies extraneous solutions. Analyzes rational functions thoroughly including end behavior. Applies rational functions to model real-world situations.
rational_11.proficient: Simplifies rational expressions. Identifies vertical and horizontal asymptotes and holes. Solves rational equations and checks for extraneous solutions. Performs operations with rational expressions.
rational_11.developing: Simplifies rational expressions with support. Identifies asymptotes and holes with guidance. Solves simple rational equations.
rational_11.beginning: Recognizes rational expressions and functions. Cannot yet reliably simplify or identify key features independently.

sequence_11.label: Sequences & Series
misconceptions: Arithmetic and geometric sequences grow at the same rate
The sum of an infinite arithmetic series is undefined
sequence_11.advanced: Analyzes arithmetic and geometric sequences and series using explicit and recursive formulas. Determines convergence of infinite series. Applies sequences and series to model and solve real-world problems. Explains why certain infinite series converge or diverge.
sequence_11.proficient: Identifies arithmetic and geometric sequences. Writes explicit and recursive formulas. Calculates finite and infinite series sums. Applies sequences to model real-world situations.
sequence_11.developing: Identifies sequence type and finds common difference/ratio. Writes explicit formulas for arithmetic sequences. Calculates sums of finite series with support.
sequence_11.beginning: Recognizes arithmetic and geometric sequences. Cannot yet reliably write formulas or calculate sums independently.
